sentences.base_language: Yusuf signed up for a cooking class at the culinary institute, something he had wanted to do for years.
sentences.target_language: سجَّل يوسف نفسه في درس طبخٍ بمعهد الفنون الطهوية، وهو أمرٌ كان يرغب فيه منذ سنوات.
sentences.romanized: Sajjala Yūsufu nafsahu fī darsi ṭabkhin bi-maʿhadi l-funūni ṭ-ṭahwiyya, wa-huwa amrun kāna yarghab fīhi mundhu sanawāt.
sentences.grammarNote:
**سجَّل** (Form II of سجَل) means to register or enroll; Form II (فعَّل) often conveys intensification or causation compared to Form I.
**كان يرغب** (kāna yarghab) is the past habitual construction — he used to desire / had been wanting; كان + مضارع expresses ongoing or habitual past states.

sentences.base_language: When he arrived, the instructor — an experienced chef named Noura — welcomed the group warmly.
sentences.target_language: حين وصل، رحَّبت المدرِّسة — وهي طاهيةٌ متمرِّسة تُدعى نورا — بالمجموعة بحفاوةٍ.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna waṣala, raḥḥabat al-mudarrisatu — wa-hiya ṭāhiyatun mutamarrisatun tudʿā Nūrā — bil-majmūʿati bi-ḥafāwa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**رحَّب بـ** (Form II of رحب) means to welcome; Form II with the preposition بـ is the fixed expression for welcoming someone — رحَّب بالضيوف = he welcomed the guests.
**تُدعى** is the passive present of دعا (to call/name), Form I passive pattern تُفعَل; the passive avoids stating who named her — she is called Noura.

sentences.base_language: She announced that they would prepare a traditional three-course meal from scratch.
sentences.target_language: أعلنت أنَّهم سيُعِدّون وجبةً تقليديةً من ثلاثة أطباق من الصفر.
sentences.romanized: Aʿlanat annuhum sa-yuʿiddūna wajbatan taqlīdiyyatan min thalāthati aṭbāqin min aṣ-ṣifr.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أنَّ** (anna) is a subordinating particle that takes a noun or pronoun subject in the accusative; أنَّهم = that they, where ـهم is the accusative/genitive pronoun suffix.
**أطباق** is the broken plural of طبق (dish/course); the pattern أفعال is one of the most common broken plural patterns in Arabic.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf had never cooked anything more complex than eggs, so he felt both excited and nervous.
sentences.target_language: لم يسبق ليوسف أن طبخ شيئاً أكثر تعقيداً من البيض، فشعر بالإثارة والتوتر معاً.
sentences.romanized: Lam yasbiq li-Yūsufa an ṭabacha shayʾan akthara taʿqīdan min al-bayḍ, fa-shaʿara bil-ithārati wa-t-tawattUri maʿan.
sentences.grammarNote:
**لم يسبق له أن** (lam yasbiq lahu an) is a set phrase meaning he had never before done something; يسبق is jussive after لم, and أن introduces the verbal complement.
**أكثر تعقيداً** is a comparative construction: أكثر + منصوب مصدر — more in terms of complexity; the masdar تعقيد is accusative as a تمييز (specification).

sentences.base_language: First, Noura demonstrated how to hold a knife correctly to avoid cutting their fingers.
sentences.target_language: أوَّلاً، أوضحت نورا كيفية الإمساك بالسكين بصورةٍ صحيحةٍ لتفادي جرح الأصابع.
sentences.romanized: Awwalan, awwaḍaḥat Nūrā kayfiyyata l-imsāki bis-sikkīni bi-ṣūratin ṣaḥīḥatin li-tafādī jurḥi l-aṣābiʿ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كيفية** (kayfiyya) is a masdar-like noun meaning the manner/way of doing something; كيفية + مصدر is a common MSA construction equivalent to how to do something.
**الإمساك** is the masdar of Form IV of مسك (to hold/grasp); إضافة constructions like كيفية الإمساك link two nouns without any preposition — the manner of holding.

sentences.base_language: She explained that most kitchen accidents happen because people do not pay attention to the blade.
sentences.target_language: شرحت أنَّ معظم حوادث المطبخ تقع لأنَّ الناس لا ينتبهون إلى النصل.
sentences.romanized: Sharaḥat anna muʿẓama ḥawādithi l-maṭbakhi taqaʿu li-anna n-nāsa lā yantabihūna ilā n-naṣl.
sentences.grammarNote:
**حوادث** is the broken plural of حادثة (accident/incident); the pattern فواعل is a common broken plural for فاعلة-pattern nouns.
**لأنَّ** (li-anna) is a compound causal conjunction (لـ + أنَّ) meaning because; like أنَّ alone, it takes its subject in the accusative — لأنَّ الناسَ = because people.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf practiced chopping onions and his eyes began to water almost immediately.
sentences.target_language: تدرَّب يوسف على تقطيع البصل فبدأت عيناه تدمعان بعد لحظاتٍ.
sentences.romanized: Tadarraba Yūsufu ʿalā taqṭīʿi l-baṣali fa-badaʾat ʿaynāhu tadmaʿāni baʿda laḥẓāt.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تدرَّب على** (Form V of درَّب) means to practice or train oneself; Form V is the reflexive of Form II — where Form II means to train someone, Form V means to train oneself.
**عيناه تدمعان** illustrates the dual: عيناه (his two eyes, nominative dual عينان + possessive ـه) governs the dual verb تدمعان (they two water), since Arabic duals take dual verb agreement.

sentences.base_language: The other students laughed kindly, and Noura said it was a sign the onions were very fresh.
sentences.target_language: ضحك الطلاب الآخرون بلطفٍ، وقالت نورا إنه دليلٌ على أنَّ البصل طازجٌ جداً.
sentences.romanized: Ḍaḥika ṭ-ṭullābu l-ākharūna bi-luṭfin, wa-qālat Nūrā innahu dalīlun ʿalā anna l-baṣala ṭāzijun jiddan.
sentences.grammarNote:
**إنَّ** (inna) is an emphatic particle taking its subject in the accusative; إنه = inna + ـه (it) reinforces the assertion — indeed it is a sign.
**الطلاب** is the broken plural of طالب (student); the pattern فُعَّال is used here, though طلاب (tuллāb) is actually the pattern فُعَال — one of the most memorized broken plurals.

sentences.base_language: For the main course, they prepared a slow-cooked stew with seasonal vegetables and tender lamb.
sentences.target_language: أعدّوا للطبق الرئيسي يخنةً تُطهى على نارٍ هادئةٍ مع الخضار الموسمية ولحم الضأن الطري.
sentences.romanized: Aʿaddū liṭ-ṭabqi r-raʾīsiyyi yakhniatan tuṭhā ʿalā nārin hādiʾatin maʿa l-khuḍāri l-mawsimiyyi wa-laḥmi ḍ-ḍaʾni ṭ-ṭariyy.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تُطهى** is a passive present Form I verb (from طَهَا, to cook); the passive Form I present pattern is تُفعَل, indicating the stew is cooked (by an unspecified agent) on low heat.
**لحم الضأن** is an إضافة (genitive construction) meaning the meat of the lamb; in إضافة the first noun (مضاف) is definite by the following noun (مضاف إليه) without needing the definite article.

sentences.base_language: Noura walked around the room, tasting each person's dish and offering quiet suggestions.
sentences.target_language: جالت نورا في أرجاء الغرفة، تتذوَّق طبق كل شخصٍ وتقدِّم اقتراحاتٍ بهدوءٍ.
sentences.romanized: Jālat Nūrā fī arjāʾi l-ghurfati, tatadhawywaqu ṭabaqa kulli shakhṣin wa-tuqaddimu iqtirāḥātin bi-hudūʾ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تتذوَّق وتقدِّم** are present tense verbs functioning as a ḥāl clause — describing what Noura was doing while walking; two simultaneous actions expressed without a conjunction introduce the second as background.
**اقتراحات** is the sound feminine plural of اقتراح (suggestion), a Form VIII masdar; Form VIII masdars (افتعال) form sound plurals with ـات.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf accidentally added too much salt but Noura told him it was an easy mistake to fix.
sentences.target_language: أضاف يوسف ملحاً أكثر مما ينبغي عن طريق الخطأ، لكنَّ نورا أخبرته أنه غلطٌ سهل التصحيح.
sentences.romanized: Aḍāfa Yūsufu milḥan akthara mimmā yanbaghī ʿan ṭarīqi l-khaṭaʾ, lakinna Nūrā akhbarathu annahu ghalatun sahlun at-taṣḥīḥ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أضاف** (Form IV of ضَوَف/ضاف) means to add; Form IV (أفعل) is regularly causative — أضاف = he caused an addition, he added.
**سهل التصحيح** is a noun-adjective phrase where التصحيح (the correction, a masdar) is the مضاف إليه of سهل in a semi-إضافة meaning easy of correction, easy to correct.

sentences.base_language: She showed him how to balance the flavors by adding a splash of lemon juice and a pinch of sugar.
sentences.target_language: أرته كيف يوازن النكهات بإضافة رشّةٍ من عصير الليمون وقرصةٍ من السكر.
sentences.romanized: Arathu kayfa yuwāzinu n-nakahhāti bi-iḍāfati rashshatin min ʿaṣīri l-laymūni wa-qarṣatin min as-sukkar.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أرى** (Form IV of رأى) means to show/make see; أرته = she showed him, where ـه is the indirect object pronoun — Form IV of رأى is causative: to make someone see.
**بإضافة** is بـ + masdar of Form IV of ضاف (إضافة, the act of adding); this بـ + مصدر structure expresses means or manner — by adding.

sentences.base_language: The aroma from the pot filled the kitchen and made everyone's mouth water.
sentences.target_language: ملأت رائحةُ القِدر المطبخَ وأثارت شهيَّةَ الجميع.
sentences.romanized: Malaʾat rāʾiḥatu l-qidri l-maṭbakha wa-athārat shahiyyata l-jamīʿ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**رائحةُ القِدر** is an إضافة where رائحة (aroma) is مضاف and القدر (the pot) is مضاف إليه in the genitive; the مضاف loses the definite article but is made definite by the following noun.
**أثارت** (Form IV of ثَوَر) means to arouse or stir up; Form IV is causative — أثار شهيّة = caused appetite to rise, i.e., made mouths water.

sentences.base_language: By the time the food was ready, Yusuf's arms were tired from all the stirring.
sentences.target_language: بحلول وقت جهوز الطعام، كانت ذراعا يوسف متعبتين من كثرة التقليب.
sentences.romanized: Bi-ḥulūli waqti juhūzi ṭ-ṭaʿāmi, kānat dhirāʿā Yūsufa mutaʿibatayni min kathraTi t-taqlīb.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ذراعا يوسف** is the dual construct (إضافة with dual مضاف): ذراعا is the dual nominative of ذراع (arm) in the genitive construct, losing its ن before the مضاف إليه — ذراعان → ذراعا يوسف.
**كثرة التقليب** is a masdar إضافة expressing the abundance of the action; كثرة (abundance, masdar of كَثُر) + التقليب (the stirring, masdar of Form II قلَّب) = all the stirring.

sentences.base_language: For dessert, they made a simple kunafa that required careful attention to the syrup temperature.
sentences.target_language: صنعوا للحلوى كنافةً بسيطةً تستوجب اهتماماً دقيقاً بدرجة حرارة القطر.
sentences.romanized: Ṣanaʿū lil-ḥalwā kanāfatan basīṭatan tastawjibu ḥtimāman daqīqan bi-darajati ḥarārati l-qaṭr.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تستوجب** (Form X of وجب) means to require or necessitate; Form X (استفعل) often expresses the requestive or the sense of finding/deeming something necessary.
**درجة حرارة** is a double إضافة chain — degree of heat of the syrup; Arabic allows multiple مضاف إليه nouns chained together: درجةُ حرارةِ القطرِ.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf felt proud when his kunafa came out of the oven perfectly golden and fragrant.
sentences.target_language: شعر يوسف بالفخر حين خرجت كنافته من الفرن ذهبيةَ اللونِ تماماً وعطرةَ الرائحة.
sentences.romanized: Shaʿara Yūsufu bil-fakhr ḥīna kharajat kanāfatuhu min al-furni dhahabiyyata l-lawni tamāman wa-ʿaṭirata r-rāʾiḥa.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ذهبيةَ اللون وعطرةَ الرائحة** are ḥāl phrases in the accusative describing the state of the kunafa as it emerged; each is a noun-noun construction (صفة مضافة) — golden of color, fragrant of aroma — a literary ḥāl pattern.
**شعر بـ** (Form I of شعر) takes the preposition بـ obligatorily — to feel something; شعر بالفخر = he felt pride (lit. he felt with pride).

sentences.base_language: The class sat down together to eat the meal they had prepared, feeling a warm sense of accomplishment.
sentences.target_language: جلس أفراد الفصل معاً ليأكلوا الوجبة التي أعدّوها، يشعرون بإحساسٍ دافئٍ بالإنجاز.
sentences.romanized: Jalasa afrādu l-faṣli maʿan li-yaʾkulū l-wajbata l-latī aʿaddūhā, yashʿurūna bi-iḥsāsin dāfiʾin bil-injāz.
sentences.grammarNote:
**التي أعدّوها** is a relative clause with a feminine relative pronoun التي referring back to الوجبة; the resumptive pronoun ـها on أعدّوها is obligatory when the relative pronoun is the object of the clause.
**يشعرون** here functions as a ḥāl clause — they sat while feeling; a present-tense verb in the nominative can serve as a ḥāl for a third-person plural subject.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf discovered that cooking was more satisfying than he had expected.
sentences.target_language: اكتشف يوسف أنَّ الطبخ أكثر إشباعاً ممّا كان يتوقّع.
sentences.romanized: Iktashafa Yūsufu anna ṭ-ṭabkha aktharu ishbāʿan mimmā kāna yatawaqquʿ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اكتشف** (Form VIII of كشف) means to discover; the Form VIII infix ـت and pattern افتعل often add a reflexive or intensive sense — كشف (to uncover) → اكتشف (to discover for oneself).
**ممّا** (min + mā) is a relative comparative — more than what; combined with كان يتوقّع (past habitual), the phrase means more than he used to/had expected.

sentences.base_language: Noura invited them all to return for the next class, which would focus on regional Arab cuisine.
sentences.target_language: دعتهم نورا جميعاً للعودة إلى الدرس التالي، الذي سيتمحور حول المأكولات العربية الإقليمية.
sentences.romanized: Daʿathum Nūrā jamīʿan lil-ʿawdati ilā d-darsi t-tālī, alladhī sa-yatamaḥwaru ḥawla l-maʾkūlāti l-ʿarabiyyati l-iqlīmiyya.
sentences.grammarNote:
**دعا + direct object + لـ** (daʿā + object + li) means to invite someone to something; لـ + masdar (للعودة = for returning) expresses the purpose of the invitation.
**يتمحور** (Form V of محور) means to revolve around / center on; Form V (تفعَّل) is the reflexive of Form II and often describes an ongoing or self-directed process.

sentences.base_language: Yusuf went home with a full stomach and a new passion, already planning what he would cook the following weekend.
sentences.target_language: عاد يوسف إلى المنزل وبطنه ممتلئةٌ وقلبه مفعمٌ بشغفٍ جديد، يضع خططاً بالفعل لما سيطبخه في عطلة نهاية الأسبوع القادم.
sentences.romanized: ʿĀda Yūsufu ilā l-manzili wa-baṭnuhu mumtaliʾatun wa-qalbuhu mafʿūmun bi-shaghafin jadīd, yaḍaʿu khuṭaṭan bil-fiʿli limā sa-yaṭbukhhu fī ʿuṭlati nihāyati l-usbūʿi l-qādim.
sentences.grammarNote:
**وبطنه ممتلئةٌ وقلبه مفعمٌ** are two ḥāl nominal clauses describing Yusuf's state as he returned; each is a small nominal sentence (مبتدأ + خبر) used circumstantially, with the ḥāl subject connected to the main subject by a possessive pronoun.
**عطلة نهاية الأسبوع** is a double إضافة chain — the holiday of the end of the week; Arabic builds compound time expressions through chained إضافة rather than compound nouns.
